note: Final adoption decision is left to the coordinator/user; this worker presents statistical facts only, per manifest adoptionRule.

resumeFromStage1Checkpoint.reason: vs_edax.py's ResultsCheckpoint.try_resume() gates on exact runKey equality (JSON-serialized settings dict, which includes opening_count/opening_limit). Stage 1's checkpoint (opening_count=3, opening_limit=3) has a different runKey than stage 2 (opening_count=30, opening_limit=None), so pointing --results-output at the stage-1 file would trigger 'runKey mismatch, starting fresh' and discard the 6 saved games rather than skip them. Removing opening_count/opening_limit from the run_key to fix this would itself require editing vs_edax.py again, which changes harnessSha256 (part of PROVENANCE_IDENTITY_KEYS) and would ALSO invalidate resume against the stage-1 checkpoint via the provenance check. A harness-migration script (same pattern as migrate_t114_exact_threshold_20.py etc.) could work around this but was judged not worth the added risk/complexity for ~170s of recompute per side.
resumeFromStage1Checkpoint.mitigation: Ran the full 30-opening command (identical to stage 1 minus --opening-limit) against new output file names (*-full.json/*-full.md), which recomputes openings 1-3 as part of one coherent run rather than skipping them. Per-game atomic checkpoint/resume-on-interruption is still fully exercised WITHIN this stage-2 invocation (would resume correctly if this run itself were interrupted, same mechanism verified in stage 1's interrupt test) — only the specific carry-over from the stage-1 file was not used.

determinismCrossCheck.method: compared stage-1 pilot's 6 games (per side) against the first 6 games of stage 2's full 30-opening run (same opening/color/weights)
determinismCrossCheck.result: exact match on margin and ply count for all 12 comparisons (6 candidate + 6 v4)

protocol.sourceOfTruth: identical to T158d stage 1 / T125 production adoption gate

execution.concurrencyAvoidedReason: wall-clock (--engine-time-ms 1500) budgeted search phase; concurrent processes would cause CPU contention and silently alter search depth/results
execution.estimateAccuracy: actual (27.4 min) came in slightly under the stage-1 estimate (28.8 min)

historicalT125V4Reference.note: informational only; different engine binary (evalCliSha256 differs), not a statistical comparison

bootstrap.method: paired bootstrap percentile CI, identical algorithm to bench/edax-compare/compare_pattern_v3.py#paired_bootstrap (random.Random(seed), resample-with-replacement len(diffs) draws per sample via rng.choice, mean each sample, sort, percentile 2.5/97.5)

gameLevel.note: supplementary finer-grained view; mean matches opening-level exactly by linearity

statistics.adoptionRuleFacts: manifest adoptionRule = 'retain production v4 absent significant or practically meaningful T158d improvement'. Point estimate (+1.45 discs/game favoring candidate) is small and positive, but both CIs straddle 0 and both sign tests are far from significant (p=0.57, p=1.00). No verdict rendered by this worker; facts only.

budgetedToExactDeviation.description: Deviation between the last budgeted-phase discDiff (empties 21-22) and the first unlimited-exact discDiff (empties 20/19), across all 120 games (60 candidate + 60 v4)
budgetedToExactDeviation.pilotFinding: candidate max 21.17 vs v4 max 5.32 (n=3 pairs)
budgetedToExactDeviation.fullRunFinding: candidate max 21.17 (same game, primary-02/black, reproduced exactly) vs v4 max 32.44 (primary-15/white) -- v4's max is now LARGER than candidate's at n=30; the pilot's apparent asymmetry was a small-sample artifact
budgetedToExactDeviation.exactFallbackTrend: pilot showed candidate 0/6 vs v4 2/6; full run reproduces and scales this: candidate 0/60 vs v4 9/60. Documented exactQuotaPercent protocol behavior, not an anomaly, but flagged as a follow-up efficiency observation.

signFlips.description: Games where budgeted-phase discDiff sign differs from the first unlimited-exact discDiff sign (i.e. apparent lead/deficit reversed by full readout)
signFlips.conclusion: identical flip count (4/60 each side); all flips originate from small near-zero budgeted values (largest magnitude 9.82 for candidate, 1.58 for v4) reversed by exact solve -- a generic property of shallow budgeted search near empties=21, not a candidate-specific defect. No catastrophic reversal (large apparent lead flipping to a large loss) observed on either side.

outOfScope: placing any weight file under train/weights/
